nodes.label: Transformer Architecture
nodes.source_file: raw/The Finetuning Landscape - A Map of Modern LLM Training.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Replaced recurrence because attention can query representations, select information dynamically, and do so in a differentiable, parallelizable way

nodes.label: Attention Mechanism
nodes.source_file: raw/The Finetuning Landscape - A Map of Modern LLM Training.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Introduced to let encoder-decoder RNNs look back at all encoder states instead of compressing input into one fixed-length vector

nodes.label: Self-Attention
nodes.source_file: raw/The Finetuning Landscape - A Map of Modern LLM Training.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: Sinusoidal Positional Encoding
nodes.source_file: raw/The Finetuning Landscape - A Map of Modern LLM Training.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Injected because attention has no notion of token order; sinusoids at different frequencies encode coarse and fine position

nodes.label: Multi-Head Attention
nodes.source_file: raw/The Finetuning Landscape - A Map of Modern LLM Training.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Multiple heads let the model jointly attend to different representation subspaces instead of averaging all relationships into one view

nodes.label: Scaled Dot-Product Attention
nodes.source_file: raw/The Finetuning Landscape - A Map of Modern LLM Training.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: Encoder-Only Transformers
nodes.source_file: raw/The Finetuning Landscape - A Map of Modern LLM Training.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Bidirectional attention suits understanding tasks: classification, retrieval, semantic similarity

nodes.label: Encoder-Decoder Transformers
nodes.source_file: raw/The Finetuning Landscape - A Map of Modern LLM Training.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Original Transformer design for sequence-to-sequence tasks like translation

nodes.label: Decoder-Only Transformers
nodes.source_file: raw/The Finetuning Landscape - A Map of Modern LLM Training.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Powers modern LLMs: simple next-token objective, massive unlabeled data, efficient parallel training make it ideal for scaling

nodes.label: Scaling Laws
nodes.source_file: raw/The Finetuning Landscape - A Map of Modern LLM Training.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Performance improves as power-law in parameters, data, and compute, so breakthroughs came from scaling rather than new architectures

nodes.label: LLM Training Pipeline
nodes.source_file: raw/The Finetuning Landscape - A Map of Modern LLM Training.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Three-stage framework (pretraining, SFT, RLHF) formalized by InstructGPT in 2022

nodes.label: Pretraining
nodes.source_file: raw/The Finetuning Landscape - A Map of Modern LLM Training.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Where the model 'learns the world'; consumes the vast majority of training compute and data

nodes.label: Causal Language Modeling
nodes.source_file: raw/The Finetuning Landscape - A Map of Modern LLM Training.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: Self-Supervised Learning
nodes.source_file: raw/The Finetuning Landscape - A Map of Modern LLM Training.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Scalable because the next token is the label, so raw internet text needs no annotation

nodes.label: Base Model
nodes.source_file: raw/The Finetuning Landscape - A Map of Modern LLM Training.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: Continued Pretraining (CPT)
nodes.source_file: raw/The Finetuning Landscape - A Map of Modern LLM Training.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Used to add domain knowledge or underrepresented languages without rebuilding a model from scratch

nodes.label: InstructGPT
nodes.source_file: raw/The Finetuning Landscape - A Map of Modern LLM Training.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: BERT
nodes.source_file: raw/The Finetuning Landscape - A Map of Modern LLM Training.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: LSTM
nodes.source_file: raw/The Finetuning Landscape - A Map of Modern LLM Training.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: Attention Is All You Need (Vaswani et al., 2017)
nodes.source_file: raw/The Finetuning Landscape - A Map of Modern LLM Training.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.norm_label: attention is all you need (vaswani et al., 2017)

nodes.label: Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models (Kaplan et al., 2020)
nodes.source_file: raw/The Finetuning Landscape - A Map of Modern LLM Training.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.norm_label: scaling laws for neural language models (kaplan et al., 2020)

nodes.label: Supervised Finetuning (SFT)
nodes.source_file: raw/The Engineer's Guide to Supervised Finetuning.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Not about adding knowledge: teaches structure, roles, boundaries and turn-taking so the model behaves like a participant instead of a text-completion engine

nodes.label: Chat Template
nodes.source_file: raw/The Engineer's Guide to Supervised Finetuning.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Translation layer (usually Jinja2 in tokenizer_config.json) between role/content messages and the flat token string; template mismatch between training and deployment breaks turn boundaries and tool calls

nodes.label: Loss Masking
nodes.source_file: raw/The Engineer's Guide to Supervised Finetuning.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Set user/system token labels to -100 (PyTorch CrossEntropyLoss ignore_index) so the model learns only to respond, not to predict the prompt

nodes.label: Sequence Packing
nodes.source_file: raw/The Engineer's Guide to Supervised Finetuning.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Concatenates documents to saturate GPUs in CPT, but in SFT can cause cross-contamination between conversations

nodes.label: Padding-Free SFT (Varlen)
nodes.source_file: raw/The Engineer's Guide to Supervised Finetuning.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Uses Flash Attention 2 with cu_seqlens to batch variable-length conversations without padding while keeping a hard firewall between them

nodes.label: Flash Attention 2
nodes.source_file: raw/The Engineer's Guide to Supervised Finetuning.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: Shift-Right Setup
nodes.source_file: raw/The Engineer's Guide to Supervised Finetuning.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Loss on the final user token predicts the first assistant token; masking off by one token breaks response initiation

nodes.label: LIMA Hypothesis
nodes.source_file: raw/The Engineer's Guide to Supervised Finetuning.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: ~1000 carefully curated high-quality examples can outperform tens of thousands of noisy ones, shifting SFT from scale to precision and data composition

nodes.label: LIMA: Less Is More for Alignment (2023)
nodes.source_file: raw/The Engineer's Guide to Supervised Finetuning.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.norm_label: lima: less is more for alignment (2023)

nodes.label: Reasoning ('Thinking') SFT
nodes.source_file: raw/The Engineer's Guide to Supervised Finetuning.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Trains the full A->B->C path with reasoning traces in <think> tags; RL cannot invent reasoning from scratch because the search space is too large, so SFT must seed the template

nodes.label: Agentic SFT
nodes.source_file: raw/The Engineer's Guide to Supervised Finetuning.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Teaches tool use via Thought->Action->Action Input->Observation->Final Response loops; format discipline (strict JSON) is as important as content

nodes.label: DeepSeek-R1
nodes.source_file: raw/The Engineer's Guide to Supervised Finetuning.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Used a cold-start SFT phase on Chain-of-Thought data before RL to seed step-by-step reasoning

nodes.label: LLM-as-a-Judge Evaluation
nodes.source_file: raw/The Engineer's Guide to Supervised Finetuning.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Low SFT loss can mean overfitting to phrasing, so a stronger teacher model grades clarity, tone, and constraint-following instead

nodes.label: IFEval Benchmark
nodes.source_file: raw/The Engineer's Guide to Supervised Finetuning.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: GAIA Benchmark
nodes.source_file: raw/The Engineer's Guide to Supervised Finetuning.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: SWE-bench
nodes.source_file: raw/The Engineer's Guide to Supervised Finetuning.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: Chain-of-Thought (CoT)
nodes.source_file: raw/The Engineer's Guide to Supervised Finetuning.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation)
nodes.source_file: raw/Understanding LoRA from First Principles.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Freezes W and learns low-rank factors B,A because finetuning updates live in a low-dimensional subspace (intrinsic rank hypothesis); avoids VRAM cost and catastrophic forgetting of full finetuning

nodes.label: Intrinsic Rank Hypothesis
nodes.source_file: raw/Understanding LoRA from First Principles.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: Autoencoder
nodes.source_file: raw/Understanding LoRA from First Principles.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Demonstrates high-dimensional data compresses into a low-dimensional latent space, the same intuition behind low-rank weight updates

nodes.label: Full Finetuning
nodes.source_file: raw/Understanding LoRA from First Principles.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Updates all weights; expensive (gradients + optimizer states) and risks catastrophic forgetting because nothing constrains updates

nodes.label: Catastrophic Forgetting
nodes.source_file: raw/Understanding LoRA from First Principles.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: Parameter-Efficient Finetuning (PEFT)
nodes.source_file: raw/Understanding LoRA from First Principles.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: LoRA Rank (r)
nodes.source_file: raw/Understanding LoRA from First Principles.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Defines the dimensionality of the update subspace; 8-32 works for most instruction tuning; trade-off is capacity vs efficiency and overfitting

nodes.label: LoRA Alpha
nodes.source_file: raw/Understanding LoRA from First Principles.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Scaling alpha/r decouples rank selection from update magnitude; common choice alpha=r or 2r

nodes.label: Zero Initialization of B
nodes.source_file: raw/Understanding LoRA from First Principles.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: BA=0 at start so the model initially behaves exactly like the base model, avoiding instability

nodes.label: LoRA Target Modules
nodes.source_file: raw/Understanding LoRA from First Principles.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: q/k/v/o_proj and MLP gate/up/down_proj; targeting all major linear layers approaches full-finetuning quality

nodes.label: Unsloth
nodes.source_file: raw/Understanding LoRA from First Principles.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: Hugging Face peft Library
nodes.source_file: raw/Understanding LoRA from First Principles.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models (2021)
nodes.source_file: raw/Understanding LoRA from First Principles.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.norm_label: lora: low-rank adaptation of large language models (2021)

nodes.label: QLoRA (Quantized LoRA)
nodes.source_file: raw/QLoRA Explained - How 4 Bit Quantization Unlocks Frontier Models.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Quantizes the frozen base model to 4-bit NF4 while keeping 16-bit LoRA adapters, freeing ~75% of weight VRAM for batch size and context without sacrificing gradient fidelity

nodes.label: The VRAM Wall
nodes.source_file: raw/QLoRA Explained - How 4 Bit Quantization Unlocks Frontier Models.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Model states (weights+gradients+optimizer) cost ~16 bytes/parameter with AdamW mixed precision; for 70B that is ~1.1TB before any tokens

nodes.label: KV Cache
nodes.source_file: raw/QLoRA Explained - How 4 Bit Quantization Unlocks Frontier Models.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Stores key/value tensors of previous tokens to avoid O(n^2) recomputation; grows with sequence length and batch size and competes with weights for VRAM

nodes.label: NF4 (4-bit NormalFloat)
nodes.source_file: raw/QLoRA Explained - How 4 Bit Quantization Unlocks Frontier Models.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Spaces its 16 quantization bins to match the zero-centered Gaussian distribution of weights, so 4-bit storage performs almost like 16-bit

nodes.label: Double Quantization
nodes.source_file: raw/QLoRA Explained - How 4 Bit Quantization Unlocks Frontier Models.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Quantizes the quantization constants themselves, saving ~0.37 bits/parameter — hundreds of MB on a 70B model

nodes.label: Paged Optimizers
nodes.source_file: raw/QLoRA Explained - How 4 Bit Quantization Unlocks Frontier Models.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Uses NVIDIA Unified Memory to offload optimizer states to CPU RAM during memory spikes, preventing OOM crashes

nodes.label: AdamW Optimizer
nodes.source_file: raw/QLoRA Explained - How 4 Bit Quantization Unlocks Frontier Models.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: Memory-Bound Inference
nodes.source_file: raw/QLoRA Explained - How 4 Bit Quantization Unlocks Frontier Models.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: GPU spends most inference time moving data from HBM, so 4-bit weights mean 4x less data movement and higher tokens/sec

nodes.label: Grouped Query Attention (GQA)
nodes.source_file: raw/QLoRA Explained - How 4 Bit Quantization Unlocks Frontier Models.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Reduces number of key/value heads to shrink KV cache footprint

nodes.label: Quantization Format Taxonomy (GGUF/GPTQ/AWQ/NVFP)
nodes.source_file: raw/QLoRA Explained - How 4 Bit Quantization Unlocks Frontier Models.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Weights-only formats (GGUF, GPTQ) dequantize for compute, solving storage but not the compute bottleneck; full quantization also targets activations

nodes.label: GPTQ
nodes.source_file: raw/QLoRA Explained - How 4 Bit Quantization Unlocks Frontier Models.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Second-order optimization minimizes quantization error layer-by-layer, but can be blind to the most critical parameters

nodes.label: AWQ (Activation-aware Weight Quantization)
nodes.source_file: raw/QLoRA Explained - How 4 Bit Quantization Unlocks Frontier Models.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Protects the ~1% of salient weights that interact with high-magnitude activations by scaling them up

nodes.label: GGUF
nodes.source_file: raw/QLoRA Explained - How 4 Bit Quantization Unlocks Frontier Models.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: MXFP4 (Microscaling)
nodes.source_file: raw/QLoRA Explained - How 4 Bit Quantization Unlocks Frontier Models.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Blocks of 32 share an 8-bit power-of-two E8M0 scale so hardware scales via bit-shifts; fast but struggles with outlier spikes

nodes.label: NVFP4
nodes.source_file: raw/QLoRA Explained - How 4 Bit Quantization Unlocks Frontier Models.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: E4M3 fractional scales with 16-element blocks hug the data distribution more tightly than MXFP4; executed natively by Blackwell Tensor Cores

nodes.label: BF16 (Brain Float 16)
nodes.source_file: raw/QLoRA Explained - How 4 Bit Quantization Unlocks Frontier Models.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Keeps FP32 dynamic range while halving size, since models care more about magnitude than decimal precision

nodes.label: FP8
nodes.source_file: raw/QLoRA Explained - How 4 Bit Quantization Unlocks Frontier Models.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: INT8 Quantization
nodes.source_file: raw/QLoRA Explained - How 4 Bit Quantization Unlocks Frontier Models.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: Tensor Cores
nodes.source_file: raw/QLoRA Explained - How 4 Bit Quantization Unlocks Frontier Models.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: NVIDIA Blackwell Architecture
nodes.source_file: raw/QLoRA Explained - How 4 Bit Quantization Unlocks Frontier Models.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Native NVFP4 execution eliminates the dequantization tax, aligning the math of the network with the silicon

nodes.label: NVIDIA Hopper (H100)
nodes.source_file: raw/QLoRA Explained - How 4 Bit Quantization Unlocks Frontier Models.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: Transformer Engine
nodes.source_file: raw/QLoRA Explained - How 4 Bit Quantization Unlocks Frontier Models.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: RLHF (Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback)
nodes.source_file: raw/The RLHF Landscape - Aligning LLMs Beyond SFT.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: SFT alone cannot close the gap between technically correct output and output humans prefer; human pairwise judgments are non-differentiable, so policy-gradient RL is needed

nodes.label: Reinforcement Learning
nodes.source_file: raw/The RLHF Landscape - Aligning LLMs Beyond SFT.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: Policy (RL)
nodes.source_file: raw/The RLHF Landscape - Aligning LLMs Beyond SFT.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: Reward Model
nodes.source_file: raw/The RLHF Landscape - Aligning LLMs Beyond SFT.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Trained on preference pairs as a scalable proxy for human judgment; its quality caps alignment quality

nodes.label: Preference Dataset
nodes.source_file: raw/The RLHF Landscape - Aligning LLMs Beyond SFT.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: PPO (Proximal Policy Optimization)
nodes.source_file: raw/The RLHF Landscape - Aligning LLMs Beyond SFT.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Default on-policy RLHF algorithm; clipped surrogate objective bounds policy updates in a trust region; requires four models in memory (actor, critic, reward, reference)

nodes.label: REINFORCE
nodes.source_file: raw/The RLHF Landscape - Aligning LLMs Beyond SFT.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Simplest policy gradient method; rarely used in production due to high variance of total-episode rewards

nodes.label: Advantage Function
nodes.source_file: raw/The RLHF Landscape - Aligning LLMs Beyond SFT.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Measures relative rather than absolute action quality to reduce gradient variance

nodes.label: Generalized Advantage Estimation (GAE)
nodes.source_file: raw/The RLHF Landscape - Aligning LLMs Beyond SFT.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: Clipped Surrogate Objective
nodes.source_file: raw/The RLHF Landscape - Aligning LLMs Beyond SFT.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Zeroes gradients when probability ratios exceed epsilon so no batch causes a catastrophic update

nodes.label: KL Divergence Penalty
nodes.source_file: raw/The RLHF Landscape - Aligning LLMs Beyond SFT.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Keeps the policy close to the SFT reference; without it models exploit reward-model quirks producing degenerate high-scoring outputs

nodes.label: DPO (Direct Preference Optimization)
nodes.source_file: raw/The RLHF Landscape - Aligning LLMs Beyond SFT.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Solves the RLHF objective in closed form, reparameterizing reward in terms of the policy: binary cross-entropy over preference pairs, no reward model or RL loop; trades alignment ceiling for cost

nodes.label: IPO (Identity Preference Optimization)
nodes.source_file: raw/The RLHF Landscape - Aligning LLMs Beyond SFT.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: DPO variant with modified loss that is more robust to overfitting

nodes.label: KTO (Kahneman-Tversky Optimization)
nodes.source_file: raw/The RLHF Landscape - Aligning LLMs Beyond SFT.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Only needs good/bad labels instead of paired comparisons, easing data collection

nodes.label: Rejection Sampling / Best-of-N
nodes.source_file: raw/The RLHF Landscape - Aligning LLMs Beyond SFT.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: RLAIF (RL from AI Feedback)
nodes.source_file: raw/The RLHF Landscape - Aligning LLMs Beyond SFT.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Replaces human annotators with an AI judge, cutting cost at the risk of inheriting judge biases

nodes.label: Direct Preference Optimization (Rafailov et al., 2023)
nodes.source_file: raw/The RLHF Landscape - Aligning LLMs Beyond SFT.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: Proximal Policy Optimization Algorithms (Schulman et al., 2017)
nodes.source_file: raw/The RLHF Landscape - Aligning LLMs Beyond SFT.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.norm_label: proximal policy optimization algorithms (schulman et al., 2017)

nodes.label: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback (Ouyang et al., 2022)
nodes.source_file: raw/The RLHF Landscape - Aligning LLMs Beyond SFT.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.norm_label: training language models to follow instructions with human feedback (ouyang et al., 2022)

nodes.label: GRPO (Group Relative Policy Optimization)
nodes.source_file: raw/The RL Algorithm Behind DeepSeek's Reasoning Models.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Eliminates the PPO critic by generating 4-8 responses per prompt and grading on a curve (score minus group mean over std); cuts memory 40-60% and training cost to ~1/18th, fitting RL on consumer GPUs; suits verifiable rewards like math/code

nodes.label: Critic (Value Model)
nodes.source_file: raw/The RL Algorithm Behind DeepSeek's Reasoning Models.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Comparable in size to the policy, doubling memory; ill-suited to LLMs where reward arrives only at sequence end

nodes.label: Group-Relative Advantage
nodes.source_file: raw/The RL Algorithm Behind DeepSeek's Reasoning Models.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: The group itself is the baseline: subtract group mean, divide by std

nodes.label: Importance Sampling Ratio
nodes.source_file: raw/The RL Algorithm Behind DeepSeek's Reasoning Models.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Corrects for reusing responses from an older policy; token-level ratios multiply over long chains and spike, injecting variance — worse in MoE due to expert-routing volatility

nodes.label: GRPO Length Bias
nodes.source_file: raw/The RL Algorithm Behind DeepSeek's Reasoning Models.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Per-token loss averaging penalizes long wrong answers less than short ones, teaching the model to ramble when wrong

nodes.label: GRPO Difficulty Bias
nodes.source_file: raw/The RL Algorithm Behind DeepSeek's Reasoning Models.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Std normalization explodes gradients when all responses in a group are right or wrong, over-indexing on trivially easy/impossible prompts

nodes.label: DAPO (Dynamic Advantage Policy Optimization)
nodes.source_file: raw/The RL Algorithm Behind DeepSeek's Reasoning Models.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Token-level gradient loss, overlong reward shaping, Clip-Higher asymmetric clipping, and dynamic sampling (each group has >=1 correct and >=1 incorrect) to fix wasted signal and instability

nodes.label: GSPO (Group Sequence Policy Optimization)
nodes.source_file: raw/The RL Algorithm Behind DeepSeek's Reasoning Models.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Shifts importance ratios to sequence level (geometric mean of token ratios) to match the sequence-level reward; eliminates Routing Replay for MoE training

nodes.label: Dr. GRPO (GRPO Done Right)
nodes.source_file: raw/The RL Algorithm Behind DeepSeek's Reasoning Models.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Removes the length and std normalization terms to produce an unbiased estimator, halting runaway incorrect-response lengths

nodes.label: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE)
nodes.source_file: raw/The RL Algorithm Behind DeepSeek's Reasoning Models.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: Routing Replay
nodes.source_file: raw/The RL Algorithm Behind DeepSeek's Reasoning Models.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Workaround freezing MoE expert paths during RL training; made unnecessary by GSPO's sequence-level ratios

nodes.label: DeepSeekMath
nodes.source_file: raw/The RL Algorithm Behind DeepSeek's Reasoning Models.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: Verifiable Rewards
nodes.source_file: raw/The RL Algorithm Behind DeepSeek's Reasoning Models.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: Vision-Language Model (VLM)
nodes.source_file: raw/Beyond Text A Guide to Vision & TTS Finetuning.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Three-stage architecture: vision encoder -> projection layer -> LLM decoder; finetuning freezes the encoder since it already extracts rich features — only domain-language mapping needs LoRA on the decoder

nodes.label: Vision Encoder
nodes.source_file: raw/Beyond Text A Guide to Vision & TTS Finetuning.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Almost always a ViT; pretrained on millions of image-text pairs, so it stays frozen during finetuning

nodes.label: Vision Transformer (ViT)
nodes.source_file: raw/Beyond Text A Guide to Vision & TTS Finetuning.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: Projection Layer
nodes.source_file: raw/Beyond Text A Guide to Vision & TTS Finetuning.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: MLP or cross-attention bridging vision embedding space to the LLM's; may compress 2x2 visual tokens to one because visual tokens are expensive context

nodes.label: Visual Tokens
nodes.source_file: raw/Beyond Text A Guide to Vision & TTS Finetuning.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: LLM-based TTS
nodes.source_file: raw/Beyond Text A Guide to Vision & TTS Finetuning.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Treats audio as just another language: the LLM predicts next audio token instead of text token, replacing the multi-stage text-normalization/phoneme/prosody/vocoder pipeline

nodes.label: Neural Audio Codec
nodes.source_file: raw/Beyond Text A Guide to Vision & TTS Finetuning.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Tokenizer for sound: encodes waveforms into discrete codebook codes and decodes them back (lossy); the key innovation enabling LLM-based TTS

nodes.label: SNAC Codec
nodes.source_file: raw/Beyond Text A Guide to Vision & TTS Finetuning.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Multi-scale codec at 24kHz with 3 hierarchical layers (12/24/48 Hz) interleaved into 7 tokens per frame, ~83 tokens/second, 4096-entry codebooks

nodes.label: Orpheus-TTS
nodes.source_file: raw/Beyond Text A Guide to Vision & TTS Finetuning.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Canopy Labs TTS on a Llama 3B backbone predicting SNAC audio tokens; emotive tags, 8 preset voices, ~200ms streaming latency; small models preferred because TTS prioritizes latency

nodes.label: Qwen3-VL
nodes.source_file: raw/Beyond Text A Guide to Vision & TTS Finetuning.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: 256K native context, DeepStack multi-level ViT feature fusion, Interleaved-MRoPE, text-timestamp alignment for video, 32-language OCR, toggleable thinking mode

nodes.label: Voice Cloning via Finetuning
nodes.source_file: raw/Beyond Text A Guide to Vision & TTS Finetuning.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Finetuning on ~30 minutes of clean single-speaker audio captures pacing and quirks that zero-shot cloning misses

nodes.label: Audio Vocabulary Extension
nodes.source_file: raw/Beyond Text A Guide to Vision & TTS Finetuning.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Audio token IDs are offset past the text vocabulary so the LLM can predict them with standard next-token prediction

nodes.label: SNAC Codec Paper (arXiv 2410.14411)
nodes.source_file: raw/Beyond Text A Guide to Vision & TTS Finetuning.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: Qwen3-VL Technical Report (arXiv 2511.21631)
nodes.source_file: raw/Beyond Text A Guide to Vision & TTS Finetuning.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: Llama 3B Backbone
nodes.source_file: raw/Beyond Text A Guide to Vision & TTS Finetuning.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: Prefill Phase
nodes.source_file: raw/A Practical Guide to LLM Inference at Scale.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Processes the whole prompt in one parallel forward pass; compute-bound; measured by Time to First Token (TTFT)

nodes.label: Decoding Phase
nodes.source_file: raw/A Practical Guide to LLM Inference at Scale.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Generates one token per step loading model weights and KV cache from HBM; memory-bandwidth bound; measured by Time Per Output Token (TPOT)

nodes.label: Time to First Token (TTFT)
nodes.source_file: raw/A Practical Guide to LLM Inference at Scale.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: Time Per Output Token (TPOT)
nodes.source_file: raw/A Practical Guide to LLM Inference at Scale.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: Static Batching
nodes.source_file: raw/A Practical Guide to LLM Inference at Scale.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Whole batch tied to the longest-running request: finished sequences idle until the longest completes, causing severe GPU underutilization

nodes.label: Continuous Batching
nodes.source_file: raw/A Practical Guide to LLM Inference at Scale.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Iteration-level scheduling: finished sequences are evicted and new requests inserted each decoding step; uses ragged batching with attention masks instead of padding

nodes.label: Chunked Prefill
nodes.source_file: raw/A Practical Guide to LLM Inference at Scale.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Splits long prompts into token-budgeted chunks piggybacked alongside decode steps to fix prefill-decode interference, at the cost of higher TTFT and repeated KV cache reloads

nodes.label: Prefill-Decode Disaggregation
nodes.source_file: raw/A Practical Guide to LLM Inference at Scale.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Runs prefill and decode on separate GPUs so each scales independently; eliminates interference but requires transferring gigabyte-scale KV caches over the network (90+ Gbps or NVLink)

nodes.label: Prefill-Decode Interference
nodes.source_file: raw/A Practical Guide to LLM Inference at Scale.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido

nodes.label: PagedAttention
nodes.source_file: raw/A Practical Guide to LLM Inference at Scale.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Inspired by OS virtual memory paging: stores the KV cache in non-contiguous fixed-size blocks, cutting memory fragmentation from ~80% to under 4% and enabling much larger batches

nodes.label: vLLM
nodes.source_file: raw/A Practical Guide to LLM Inference at Scale.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Combines continuous batching with PagedAttention for up to 23-24x throughput over naive static batching

nodes.label: kvcached
nodes.source_file: raw/A Practical Guide to LLM Inference at Scale.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
nodes.rationale: Daemon decoupling GPU virtual addressing from physical allocation so multiple LLMs elastically share GPU memory; 2-28x TTFT reduction on bursty workloads

nodes.label: Ragged Batching
nodes.source_file: raw/A Practical Guide to LLM Inference at Scale.md
nodes.author: Miguel Otero Pedrido
